heading: Write-Blocking Prevention
body:
To prevent write-blocking behavior from occurring and bring the cluster
below the Atlas write-blocking [write-blocking-thresholds](write-blocking-thresholds),
we recommend that you:

- Enable [storage auto-scaling](howitworks-scale-cluster-storage),
  which automatically scales your cluster storage when disk utilization
  exceeds 90% and ensures consistent workload availability and performance.

  To enable storage auto-scaling in the Atlas UI, navigate to
  the **Cluster Tier** menu and check the **Storage Scaling**
  checkbox in the **Auto-scale** section.

  - For all new clusters that you create in the Atlas UI,
    Atlas enables storage auto-scaling by default.

  - If you [create clusters with the API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupcluster),
    cluster auto-scaling isn't selected by default and you must explicitly enable it,
    using the options in the `autoScaling` object of the [Update One Cluster in One Project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupcluster) endpoint.

- If you choose to opt out of storage auto-scaling and your disk utilization
  is close to 90%, you can release existing disk space before Atlas
  activates write-blocking behavior on your cluster:

  - Remove unused data to free up disk space. [Delete the indexes](manual/core/indexes/drop-index),
    or [delete collections or individual documents](manual/tutorial/remove-documents).

  - Use the [autoCompact](manual/reference/command/autoCompact) command on each dedicated cluster node
    to compact disk space.
